changes.before: "Did you." Sylvanas's voice stayed mild. "There were drawers of hands. A room of bodies still breathing on tables. Another with parts sorted into crates. He was teaching you over that floor."
changes.after: "Did you. There were drawers of hands. A room of bodies still breathing on tables. Another with parts sorted into crates. He was teaching you over that floor."

changes.before: That struck lower than anger would have. Jaina stared at the muddy seam between two wheel tracks until it blurred.
changes.after: Jaina stared at the muddy seam between two wheel tracks until it blurred.

changes.before: Jaina understood that she had chosen the omission hours ago in the sanctum and again on the island and again when the road began.
changes.after: She had chosen the omission hours ago in the sanctum and again on the island and again when the road began.

changes.before: Tell them I am not riding north while the road south is still moving.
changes.after: Tell them I am not riding north while traffic is still moving south.

changes.before: "Jaina." His voice came thin with range and strain, the careful cadence pared down so hard the care showed as absence.
changes.after: "Jaina." His voice came thin with range and strain.

changes.before: Antonidas had always done more with silence than other men did with speeches.
Wind hissed through the orchard hedges. Cyndia's mare tossed foam from the bit and settled. Sylvanas gave no sign of listening, but Jaina knew she was taking every word.
changes.after: Wind hissed through the orchard hedges. Cyndia's mare tossed foam from the bit and settled. Sylvanas gave no sign of listening, but she was taking every word.

changes.before: For a moment Antonidas sounded dry and impatient again.
changes.after: Antonidas sounded dry and impatient again.

changes.before: He would. Too often, what he could move arrived late.
Jaina hated the look on the man's face because she already knew what shape it meant.
changes.after: Jaina hated the look on the man's face.

changes.before: Night slid up from the ditches.
The warmth drained out of the fields.
Truth from Sylvanas was a blade; you took the cut cleanly or made it worse by flinching into it.
Perhaps only in pieces. Perhaps too late for half the city.
Jaina hated the look on the man's face because it carried the answer already.
changes.after: Jaina hated the look on the man's face.
